testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.title: Epic Overview — Full Implementation Plan
userStories.overview:
Currently, epics are managed through modals (`EpicModal.tsx`) and sidebar list items. There's no dedicated page to view an epic's full picture — description, progress, tickets, learnings, and git info. The ticket detail page (`ticket.$id.tsx`) serves as a proven template for this pattern.
The `epicWorkflowState` table already tracks rich data (ticketsTotal, ticketsDone, currentTicketId, learnings, branch/PR info) that isn't surfaced anywhere in a consolidated view.
userStories.description:
# Epic Detail Page — Full Implementation Plan

**Shared feature branch:** All tickets in this epic share a single feature branch.

## Context

Currently, epics are managed through modals (`EpicModal.tsx`) and sidebar list items. There's no dedicated page to view an epic's full picture — description, progress, tickets, learnings, and git info. The ticket detail page (`ticket.$id.tsx`) serves as a proven template for this pattern.

The `epicWorkflowState` table already tracks rich data (ticketsTotal, ticketsDone, currentTicketId, learnings, branch/PR info) that isn't surfaced anywhere in a consolidated view.

## Page Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ <- Back                                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [color dot] Epic Title                     [Edit] [Launch▾] │
│ Project: Name  ·  12 tickets  ·  75% complete               │
│ Branch: feature/epic-abc  ·  PR #42 (draft)                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Progress                                                     │
│ [████████████████████░░░░░░░] 75%                           │
│ ● Backlog 1  ● Ready 2  ● In Progress 1  ● Done 9          │
│ Currently working on: "Add auth middleware"                  │
├──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┤
│ Description              │ Tickets                          │
│ (markdown, reuses        │ ▸ In Progress (1)                │
│  TicketDescription)      │   ○ Ticket A - High [PR #5]      │
│                          │ ▸ Ready (2)                       │
│                          │   ○ Ticket B - Medium             │
│                          │   ○ Ticket C - Low                │
│                          │ ▸ Done (9) [collapsed]            │
├──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┤
│ Learnings (collapsible)                                      │
│ From "Add Auth" (3 learnings):                              │
│   [pattern] Use middleware for auth checks                   │
│   [anti-pattern] Don't store tokens in localStorage          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Created: Jan 15, 2026                                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Files to Create

### 1. `src/routes/epic.$id.tsx` — Route component
- Follow `ticket.$id.tsx` pattern: `createFileRoute("/epic/$id")`, error component, loading skeleton
- Fetch data with `useEpicDetail(id)` hook
- Back navigation with `useCanGoBack()`
- Edit via existing `EpicModal`
- Launch actions: full dropdown (Claude, Codex, VS Code, Ralph, etc.) reusing `handleStartInteractive` pattern from `EpicModal` — launches the next non-done ticket

### 2. `src/components/epics/EpicDetailHeader.tsx` — Header
- Epic title, color dot, edit button, launch dropdown
- Badge row: project name, ticket count, completion percentage
- Git row: branch name (copyable), PR link with status badge
- Reuses `STATUS_BADGE_CONFIG` from `src/lib/constants.ts` for PR status

### 3. `src/components/epics/EpicProgressOverview.tsx` — Progress section
- Multi-segment progress bar color-coded by status
- Status chips with per-status ticket counts
- "Currently working on" indicator when `currentTicketId` is set
- Uses colors from `STATUS_BADGE_CONFIG`

### 4. `src/components/epics/EpicTicketsList.tsx` — Tickets grouped by status
- Tickets grouped by status (in_progress first, done last)
- Each ticket: title (links to `/ticket/$id`), priority badge, blocked indicator, PR status
- Status groups are collapsible, "done" group collapsed by default
- Empty state when no tickets

### 5. `src/components/epics/EpicLearnings.tsx` — Learnings section
- Collapsible panel
- Grouped by source ticket
- Type badges: pattern (green), anti-pattern (red), tool-usage (blue), workflow (purple)
- Empty state: "No learnings yet"

## Files to Modify

### 6. `src/api/epics.ts` — Add `getEpicDetail` server function
- Single composite fetch: epic + project + tickets (partial fields) + epicWorkflowState + learnings
- Returns `EpicDetailResult` type with everything the page needs
- Computes `ticketsByStatus` breakdown server-side
- Falls back to ticket array counts when `epicWorkflowState` doesn't exist

### 7. `src/lib/query-keys.ts` — Add epic detail key
```ts
epicDetail: (epicId: string) => ["epic", epicId] as const,
```

### 8. `src/lib/hooks/projects.ts` — Add `useEpicDetail` hook
- Wraps `getEpicDetail` with `useQuery`
- `staleTime: 0` (can be updated externally via MCP)

### 9. `src/lib/hooks/index.ts` — Re-export `useEpicDetail`

### 10. `src/components/epics/index.ts` — Export new components

### 11. `src/components/navigation/EpicListItem.tsx` — Add detail page link
- Keep current click behavior (filters the board)
- Add a secondary icon/button to navigate to `/epic/$id`

## Key Reuse

| Existing Code | Reuse For |
|---|---|
| `TicketDescription` component | Render epic markdown description as-is |
| `EpicModal` component | Edit epic (title, description, color) — already fully built |
| `STATUS_BADGE_CONFIG` / `PRIORITY_BADGE_CONFIG` | Status/priority badge styling |
| `handleStartInteractive` pattern from `EpicModal` | Launch AI on next non-done ticket |
| `useCanGoBack` / back nav pattern from `ticket.$id.tsx` | Back button |
| `getEpicContext` / `getTicketContext` from `src/api/context.ts` | Context for launching |
| `ensureExists` from `src/lib/utils` | Server function error handling |

## Data Flow

```
/epic/{id} → useEpicDetail(id) → getEpicDetail server fn
                                    ├─ SELECT epic WHERE id = ?
                                    ├─ SELECT project WHERE id = epic.projectId
                                    ├─ SELECT tickets (partial) WHERE epicId = ?
                                    ├─ SELECT epicWorkflowState WHERE epicId = ?
                                    └─ JSON.parse(learnings)
                                  → EpicDetailResult distributed to components
```

## Navigation Integration

- Sidebar epic click **keeps current behavior** (filters the board)
- Add a secondary icon/button on `EpicListItem` to navigate to `/epic/$id`
- Ticket detail page's epic badge could link to `/epic/$id`
- No new sidebar nav item needed

## Verification

1. `pnpm type-check` — no errors
2. `pnpm lint` — no errors
3. `pnpm test` — all pass
4. Manual browser testing at `localhost:4242`:
   - Navigate to `/epic/{id}` directly
   - Click detail icon on epic list item → lands on detail page
   - Verify progress bar matches actual ticket statuses
   - Click ticket in list → navigates to ticket detail
   - Edit epic via Edit button → modal opens, saves correctly
   - Launch actions work (launches next non-done ticket)
   - Back button works (browser history and fallback)
   - Loading skeleton displays briefly
   - Error page shows for invalid epic IDs
   - Learnings section shows/hides correctly

userStories.title: Backend: getEpicDetail server function + useEpicDetail hook
acceptanceCriteria: `getEpicDetail` returns all data needed for the Epic Detail page in one call
Returns proper error (via `ensureExists`) for non-existent epic IDs
Learnings JSON parse failure returns empty array (not error)
userStories.description:
**Shared feature branch** — Part of the Epic Detail Page epic. All tickets share one branch.

## Goal
Create the backend data layer that powers the Epic Detail page — a single composite server function and corresponding React Query hook.

## Files to Modify

### `src/api/epics.ts` — Add `getEpicDetail` server function
- New `EpicDetailResult` interface containing: epic, project, tickets (partial fields), ticketsByStatus breakdown, workflowState, learnings
- Single server function that fetches everything in one call (no client-side waterfall)
- Import `epicWorkflowState` from `src/lib/schema.ts`
- Import `tickets`, `projects` from schema
- Compute `ticketsByStatus` record server-side by iterating tickets
- Gracefully handle missing `epicWorkflowState` row (fall back to computed values from ticket array)
- Parse learnings JSON from workflow state with try/catch fallback to empty array
- Use `ensureExists` from `src/lib/utils` for epic and project lookups
- Ticket fields to select: id, title, status, priority, isBlocked, blockedReason, branchName, prNumber, prUrl, prStatus

### `src/lib/query-keys.ts` — Add query key
```ts
epicDetail: (epicId: string) => ["epic", epicId] as const,
```

### `src/lib/hooks/projects.ts` — Add `useEpicDetail` hook
- Import `getEpicDetail` from `src/api/epics`
- `staleTime: 0` to match ticket detail pattern (data can be updated externally via MCP)
- Return `{ data, loading, error, refetch }`

### `src/lib/hooks/index.ts` — Re-export
- Add `useEpicDetail` and `EpicDetailResult` type to the barrel exports

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `getEpicDetail` returns all data needed for the Epic Detail page in one call
- [ ] Returns proper error (via `ensureExists`) for non-existent epic IDs
- [ ] `ticketsByStatus` correctly counts tickets per status
- [ ] Missing `epicWorkflowState` returns null (not error)
- [ ] Learnings JSON parse failure returns empty array (not error)
- [ ] `useEpicDetail` hook works with React Query
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes

## Branch Strategy
**All tickets in this epic work on the same branch:**  (branched from `feature/epic-b65163de-align-provider-setup-scripts-with-post-refactor-ar`). Do NOT create a new branch — commit directly to this existing branch so all changes can be tested together before merging to feature/epic-b65163de-align-provider-setup-scripts-with-post-refactor-ar

userStories.description:
**Shared feature branch** — Part of the Epic Detail Page epic. All tickets share one branch.

## Goal
Create the `EpicProgressOverview` component that shows a visual progress bar and per-status ticket breakdown.

## File to Create

### `src/components/epics/EpicProgressOverview.tsx`

**Props:**
```ts
interface EpicProgressOverviewProps {
  ticketsByStatus: Record<string, number>;
  ticketsTotal: number;
  ticketsDone: number;
  currentTicketId: string | null;
  currentTicketTitle?: string;
}
```

**Features:**
- Multi-segment progress bar where each segment is color-coded by status
- Use colors from `STATUS_BADGE_CONFIG` in `src/lib/constants.ts`
- Status order: backlog → ready → in_progress → ai_review → human_review → done
- Status chips below the bar showing count per status (only show statuses with count > 0)
- Percentage label (ticketsDone / ticketsTotal)
- "Currently working on: [ticket title]" indicator when `currentTicketId` is set
- Handle edge case: ticketsTotal === 0 (show "No tickets" empty state)
- CSS variables for styling consistency (`var(--spacing-*)`, `var(--bg-*)`, etc.)

**Depends on:** Backend ticket (needs `EpicDetailResult` type)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Progress bar renders with correct proportions per status
- [ ] Status chips show correct counts
- [ ] Percentage displays correctly
- [ ] Current ticket indicator shows when applicable
- [ ] Empty state when no tickets
- [ ] Uses existing `STATUS_BADGE_CONFIG` colors
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes

## Branch Strategy
**All tickets in this epic work on the same branch:**  (branched from `feature/epic-b65163de-align-provider-setup-scripts-with-post-refactor-ar`). Do NOT create a new branch — commit directly to this existing branch so all changes can be tested together before merging to feature/epic-b65163de-align-provider-setup-scripts-with-post-refactor-ar

userStories.description:
**Shared feature branch** — Part of the Epic Detail Page epic. All tickets share one branch.

## Goal
Create the `EpicTicketsList` component that shows all tickets in the epic, grouped by status with collapsible sections.

## File to Create

### `src/components/epics/EpicTicketsList.tsx`

**Props:**
```ts
interface EpicTicketsListProps {
  tickets: Array<{
    id: string;
    title: string;
    status: string;
    priority: string | null;
    isBlocked: boolean | null;
    blockedReason: string | null;
    prNumber: number | null;
    prStatus: string | null;
  }>;
}
```

**Features:**
- Group tickets by status in order: in_progress, ai_review, human_review, ready, backlog, done
- Each group has a collapsible header showing status label + count (e.g., "In Progress (3)")
- "Done" group is collapsed by default, all others expanded
- Each ticket row shows:
  - Title as a `Link` to `/ticket/$id` (TanStack Router)
  - Priority badge using `PRIORITY_BADGE_CONFIG` from `src/lib/constants.ts`
  - Blocked indicator (icon + tooltip with reason) when `isBlocked` is true
  - PR status badge when `prNumber` is set
- Status group headers use colors from `STATUS_BADGE_CONFIG`
- Empty state: "No tickets in this epic yet"
- Use ChevronDown/ChevronRight from lucide-react for collapse toggles

**Depends on:** Backend ticket (needs ticket data shape)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tickets correctly grouped by status
- [ ] Groups appear in priority order (active statuses first)
- [ ] "Done" group collapsed by default
- [ ] Click ticket title navigates to `/ticket/$id`
- [ ] Priority badges display correctly
- [ ] Blocked tickets show indicator
- [ ] PR status shows when applicable
- [ ] Empty state renders when no tickets
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes

## Branch Strategy
**All tickets in this epic work on the same branch:**  (branched from `feature/epic-b65163de-align-provider-setup-scripts-with-post-refactor-ar`). Do NOT create a new branch — commit directly to this existing branch so all changes can be tested together before merging to feature/epic-b65163de-align-provider-setup-scripts-with-post-refactor-ar

userStories.description:
**Shared feature branch** — Part of the Epic Detail Page epic. All tickets share one branch.

## Goal
Create the `EpicDetailHeader` component showing the epic title, metadata badges, git/PR info, and action buttons (edit + launch).

## File to Create

### `src/components/epics/EpicDetailHeader.tsx`

**Layout:**
```
[color dot] Epic Title                          [Edit] [Launch ▾]
Project: Name  ·  12 tickets  ·  75% complete
Branch: feature/epic-abc  ·  PR #42 (draft)
```

**Features:**
- **Title row:** Epic color dot, h1 title, Edit button, Launch split-button dropdown
- **Badge row:** Project name badge, ticket count badge, completion percentage badge
- **Git row** (conditional, only when workflowState exists):
  - Branch name with copy-to-clipboard (reuse clipboard pattern from `TicketDetailHeader`)
  - PR link with status badge (draft/open/merged/closed) when `prNumber` is set
- **Edit button:** Opens existing `EpicModal` component (passed via `onEdit` callback)
- **Launch dropdown:** Full launch menu matching `EpicModal`'s action menu structure:
  - Interactive section: Claude, Codex (auto/cli/app), VS Code, Cursor, Copilot CLI, OpenCode
  - Ralph section: Claude, Codex, VS Code, Cursor, Copilot CLI, OpenCode
  - Each launches the next non-done ticket in the epic (same `handleStartInteractive` / `handleStartRalph` pattern from `EpicModal.tsx`)

**Pattern references:**
- `src/components/tickets/TicketDetailHeader.tsx` — badge row, git info, clipboard copy
- `src/components/EpicModal.tsx` lines 295-385 — `handleStartInteractive` launch logic
- `src/components/EpicModal.tsx` lines 195-292 — `handleStartRalph` launch logic
- `src/lib/constants.ts` — `STATUS_BADGE_CONFIG` for PR status styling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Title displays with color dot
- [ ] Badge row shows project, ticket count, completion %
- [ ] Git row shows branch and PR when available
- [ ] Edit button triggers onEdit callback
- [ ] Launch dropdown opens with full Interactive + Ralph sections
- [ ] Launch actions work (launch next non-done ticket)
- [ ] Copy branch name to clipboard works
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes

## Branch Strategy
**All tickets in this epic work on the same branch:**  (branched from `feature/epic-b65163de-align-provider-setup-scripts-with-post-refactor-ar`). Do NOT create a new branch — commit directly to this existing branch so all changes can be tested together before merging to feature/epic-b65163de-align-provider-setup-scripts-with-post-refactor-ar

userStories.description:
**Shared feature branch** — Part of the Epic Detail Page epic. All tickets share one branch.

## Goal
Create the main route page that assembles all epic detail components, and add navigation links from the sidebar and ticket detail page.

## Files to Create

### `src/routes/epic.$id.tsx` — Main route

Follow `src/routes/ticket.$id.tsx` pattern exactly:

**Route definition:**
```ts
export const Route = createFileRoute("/epic/$id")({
  component: EpicDetailPage,
  errorComponent: EpicDetailError,
});
```

**Error component:** `EpicDetailError` — shows "Epic Not Found" with back navigation (same pattern as `TicketDetailError`)

**Loading skeleton:** `EpicDetailSkeleton` — placeholder UI with pulse animation mimicking the page layout

**Main component:** `EpicDetailPage` — assembles:
1. Back navigation (useCanGoBack + router.history.back or fallback to /board)
2. `EpicDetailHeader` — title, badges, git info, launch actions
3. `EpicProgressOverview` — progress bar and status breakdown
4. Content grid (2 columns):
   - Left: `TicketDescription` (reused from `src/components/tickets/`) with `epic.description`
   - Right: `EpicTicketsList` with tickets array
5. `EpicLearnings` — collapsible learnings panel
6. Footer with created date
7. `EpicModal` (rendered conditionally for edit)

**Data fetching:**
- `useEpicDetail(id)` for all epic data
- `useTickets({ epicId: id })` for launch actions (finding next non-done ticket)
- `useSettings()` for terminal preferences
- `useLaunchRalphForEpic()` for Ralph launches

**State:**
- `showEditModal` boolean
- Launch state (isLaunching, notification — same pattern as EpicModal)

**Cache invalidation:** After edit (onSave from EpicModal), invalidate `queryKeys.epicDetail(id)` and `queryKeys.projectsWithEpics`

## Files to Modify

### `src/components/navigation/EpicListItem.tsx` — Add detail link
- Keep current `onSelect` click behavior (filters the board)
- Add a secondary icon button (e.g., `ExternalLink` or `ArrowRight` from lucide-react) that navigates to `/epic/$id`
- Use TanStack Router's `Link` component or `useNavigate`

### `src/components/epics/index.ts` — Export new components
- Add exports for: `EpicDetailHeader`, `EpicProgressOverview`, `EpicTicketsList`, `EpicLearnings`

**Depends on:** All previous component tickets in this epic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Route `/epic/{id}` renders the full epic detail page
- [ ] Loading skeleton shows during data fetch
- [ ] Error page shows for invalid epic IDs
- [ ] Back button navigates correctly (history or fallback)
- [ ] All sections render with correct data
- [ ] Edit modal opens and saves correctly
- [ ] Launch actions work end-to-end
- [ ] EpicListItem has secondary link to detail page
- [ ] Barrel exports updated
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes
- [ ] Manual browser verification at localhost:4242

## Branch Strategy
**All tickets in this epic work on the same branch:**  (branched from `feature/epic-b65163de-align-provider-setup-scripts-with-post-refactor-ar`). Do NOT create a new branch — commit directly to this existing branch so all changes can be tested together before merging to feature/epic-b65163de-align-provider-setup-scripts-with-post-refactor-ar

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicDescription: Add a dedicated Epic Detail page (`/epic/$id`) that provides a comprehensive view of an epic — its description, progress breakdown, associated tickets, learnings, and git/PR info. Currently epics are only viewable through modals and sidebar items with no consolidated detail view.\n\nAll tickets in this epic share a single feature branch.
